political.s: Brown-Forman maintains a Jack Daniel's PAC and makes corporate political contributions to state and local candidates. The company has engaged in political giving but specific PAC amounts are not disclosed in available sources.

charity.s: Brown-Forman committed $22.5 million to the Brown-Forman Foundation and Dendrifund in 2024, supporting arts, education, sustainable living, and environmental initiatives. The company established its foundation in 2018 and focuses primarily on Louisville, Kentucky.
charity.focus: Arts and culture, education, sustainable living, environmental conservation

environment.s: Brown-Forman has committed to reducing greenhouse gas emissions and achieving net positive water impact. However, Scope 3 emissions increased 26% since 2022, and absolute GHG emissions have increased despite efficiency improvements. The company diverts 99.7% of owned facility waste from landfill.

labor.s: Brown-Forman was found by the NLRB to have committed unfair labor practices at its Woodford Reserve facility, implementing compensatory raises and benefits after learning of union organizing. The Sixth Circuit upheld the unfair labor finding but rejected the bargaining order on procedural grounds in March 2026.

dei.s: Brown-Forman ended its diversity and inclusion programs in August 2024, removing quantitative workforce diversity goals and ceasing participation in the Human Rights Campaign's Corporate Equality Index after pressure from anti-DEI activists. The company had previously achieved perfect scores on HRC benchmarks.

animals.s: Not applicable to spirits production and distribution.

guns.s: Not applicable to spirits production and distribution.

privacy.s: No significant data privacy breaches reported in available sources. Company privacy practices not extensively documented in public records.

execPay.s: CEO Lawson Whiting's total compensation is $13.45 million, approximately average for companies of similar size. Compensation is tied to business performance. Previously 10% of executive short-term compensation was linked to DEI goals but this has been removed.
execPay.ratio: Approximately 113:1 (estimated based on average employee salary of ~$95-119k vs CEO pay of $13.45M)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
